rootCause: validateConnectionConfig() calls tryLocalPairing() which saves the bridge token to chrome.storage.local, but the local config object passed to connectWebSocket() is not updated. When socket.open fires, extension.hello is sent with config.token (empty string), causing the bridge to reject with invalid_token (PolicyViolation close code 1008). The reconnect loop persists because each reconnect attempt re-enters the same code path with the same stale empty-token config object.
fixApplied: validateConnectionConfig() now returns the effective token (from pairing or from config); connectWebSocket() captures this as effectiveToken and sends it in extension.hello instead of config.token. Fixes the stale-closure bug where tryLocalPairing() result was discarded.
